title: Chapter 47: Descent
content: The entrance to the 11th Layer was beneath the Hollow Cathedral.
Roxanne had been here before — the underground space where the veil between worlds was thinnest. But she had never gone deeper. The sealed gateway at the cathedral's heart had always been locked.
Now it was open.
"The First Seat has already begun," Frost said, examining the broken seals. "The gate is unsealed. Something is coming through."
"How long?"
"Hours. Maybe less."
Roxanne looked at her team: Silas, Frost, the Executioner, Kallos, and behind them, a dozen volunteers from the Crossroads who had refused to stay behind.
"You don't have to come," she told them.
"We know," Kallos said. "We're coming anyway."
She nodded.
"Let's go."

The descent took them through layers of reality that defied description. Each step down was a step into a darker world. The violet light of the surface Nether-Grid faded, replaced by a deep crimson that seemed to pulse like a heartbeat.
"The 11th Layer was never meant to be traversed by mortals," Silas said. "The pressure alone —"
"I'm fine."
"You're bleeding from your nose."
Roxanne wiped her upper lip. Her hand came away red.
"I said I'm fine."
They walked deeper.
The air grew thick. Heavy. Each breath was an effort.
And then they reached the bottom.
The Hell Gate stood before them.

It was not a gate in the traditional sense. It was a wound in reality — a vertical tear in the fabric of existence, edged with teeth. Rows of teeth. Human teeth. Animal teeth. Teeth that had never belonged to any living thing.
And before the gate stood the First Seat.
"You came," it said. "I knew you would."
"You wanted me to."
"Of course. I cannot destroy the restored Void directly. But I can destroy its heir. And without an heir, the Void will collapse again."
Roxanne looked at the teeth-lined gate.
"You opened the Hell Gate just to lure me here."
"I opened the Hell Gate because I will destroy everything before I let you win."
"That's not strategy. That's a tantrum."
The First Seat's form flickered.
"Call it what you wish. It will still kill you."
